programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A covert identity created for one of Gibbs' undercover cases 20 years previously resurfaces after a murder victim is found to be using the alias. Meanwhile, Bishop becomes anxious at the thought of having to work incognito in the future. Starring Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: While tracking a device that can prevent nuclear meltdown through the sewers, Walter learns the nuances of listening and not offering advice to Paige.

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. programme.desc: Part one of two. Dr Sloan and Amanda help the FBI investigate a plane crash near the hospital, but just as they think the case is solved, Jesse announces two bodies have gone missing. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mark recalls an incident from 20 years previously that appears to hold the key to solving the case involving cover-ups, radioactive fuel and the death of an airline rep. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A TV executive is murdered and the subsequent death of the chief suspect makes Mark and Steve realise a serial killer is on the loose. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse's quiet fishing trip with his estranged father takes an unexpected turn thanks to revelations about international espionage. Drama, guest starring Patrick Macnee, Robert Vaughn, Phil Morris and Robert Culp

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Randy thwarts Steve's efforts to catch a thief and claims a mime artist mysteriously appeared in her bed - but by the time the Sloans arrive he has gone. Ken Kercheval guest stars

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The story of a blimp that crashed during the Second World War, with the crew nowhere to be found. Plus, the hunt for a serial killer in New Orleans

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: Accounts of a ghostly submarine from the Second World War. Plus, a look at the work of psychic investigator Nancy Myer

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The mysterious death of 1960s music producer Bobby Fuller. Plus, strange cases involving twins who were separated at birth

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries

programme.desc: The attorney gets involved when Michelle takes on the case of an old friend who killed her lover

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: A professor is murdered, and suspicion falls on a student he was having an affair with

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: There is no shortage of suspects when a talk show host is murdered

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: The attorney tries to prove that a paraplegic man is guilty of murdering his wife

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Michelle and Conrad discover Matlock has been kidnapped, but realise one of his old cases might hold the key to figuring out where he is being held

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the mysterious death of a man torn apart by tigers at the Bronx Zoo. They are led to both the victim's meat-packing plant as well as his Mafia relatives. Hawkes and Stella take on the case of a debutante found dead on a carousel only hours before her big night. Drama, starring Melina Kanakaredes, Gary Sinise, Hill Harper and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and the team are sent to investigate the case of two people killed at an oil company whose executives are martial arts experts. Meanwhile, Dr Hawkes and Detective Flack look into the suspicious death of a teenage boy who spent most of his time on his computer. Drama, starring Gary Sinise, Eddie Cahill and Hill Harper

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Stella and Lindsay discover a link between the deaths of a tram driver and a dancer who recently won the lottery. Meanwhile, Danny and Hawkes investigate the murder of a fish merchant and are led to a jealous friend who is resentful that his child was refused entry to a prestigious school

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Danny, Flack and Lindsay investigate the case of a middle-aged tycoon killed in a lift, and discover a world in which girls meet much older men for sexual encounters. Stella and Hawkes uncover the identity of a body found in Central Park, learning the victim had been living a double life which ultimately led to his death. Drama, starring Gary Sinise

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Miami detective Horatio Caine enlists Mac's help as he pursues serial killer Henry Darius in New York. However, he soon loses the trail and is forced to follow other leads. An investigation into the sister of one of Darius's victims proves a dead end, but the team soon finds a vital link between the murderer and one of his hostages. David Caruso guest stars

programme.desc: When Tracey decides to organise a meeting of the Prisoners' Wives Support Group, Sharon gets more involved than she expected. Comedy, starring Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon decides to give the cafe a new image and cut all connections with the Greek community - but the radical changes don't have the desired effect. Comedy, with Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Just two days away from retirement, June uncovers a swindle involving property developer Brian Stevens (guest star Mike Reid) and a local authority official involved in the sell-off of the Aldbourne estate. The trail leads close to home and, when DAC Georgia Hobbs is seen kissing John Heaton, June is left not knowing who she can trust

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Annalese is suspended as part of the inquiry into Steve Hewitt's death and finds her whole world falling apart as she seeks advice from a solicitor. Ric demands Tom prove he is up to being a surgeon after dismissing his report as a tissue of lies and telling him to produce another, while Jac sees an opportunity for advancement when she assists Elliot in operating secretly on Samson the dog

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Roxy goes into premature labour, causing Nikki to put her grievances aside to help deliver the baby in the back of her car - but panic sets in when the newborn has difficulty breathing. Tally's jealousy gets the better of her as she proceeds to scratch Lara's car with a key and hurl a brick through Simon's window. Guest starring Norman Pace

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June and Gina become convinced of Heaton's involvement in the property development conspiracy when he takes charge, announcing he will deal with Georgia in his own way. Evictions begin on the Aldbourne estate and Nikki faces a protest which escalates into a riot when fire bombs are thrown. A familiar face turns up at Sun Hill to see June clock off for the last time

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Set in a close-knit community in London's East End, these are the beginnings of Britain's favourite soap; intertwining relationships, social issues, triumphs, tragedies, loves and losses

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia expresses her reservations about the artist Lloyd Ellis Parker to James, while Rene is distraught to learn about Sarah's pregnancy. Meanwhile, Carolyn confronts George about Regina, and Elizabeth has lunch with Douglas Goddard

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: The Mayor of Cardale suffers severe attacks of lethargy, and Tom uncovers an affair between a local teacher and one of her pupils _ a revelation that sets him thinking about his own feelings for Alex. Medical drama, starring Gray O'Brien and Maggie O'Neill, with a guest appearance by Roy Barraclough

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Herriots give Highland dancing a fling, while Siegfried catches fleas and a stag disappears. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Lynda Bellingham and Robert Hardy

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Sandy has relationship problems and Jean frets when Judith starts dating a married man. At a dinner party attended by Penny and Stephen, Lionel has to steer the conversation away from personal matters. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel's peaceful weekend in the country is disrupted when they find the faithful Mrs Bale is not her usual cheerful self. To add to their woes, Lionel feels unwell - but a visit from a doctor with a strange bedside manner sheds light on their housekeeper's mood

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth develops a passion for art, Rose acquires a new admirer, and Daddy has a yearning to join the Foreign Legion. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg, Truly, Alvin and Billy encounter a stranger who is determined to travel by canal to Ilkley in an inflatable swan. However, when punctures disrupt the man's dream, Entwistle is on hand to ensure the voyage goes to plan. Tommy Cannon and Bobby Ball guest star

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: The theft and subsequent criminal misuse of DC Tomlin's warrant card adds to DI Raine's troubles as she identifies the body of a local drugs dealer and faces an outbreak of gang warfare on her patch - and it's not long before a second corpse is retrieved from the canal. Police drama, starring Pauline Quirke and Steve John Shepherd

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: When a toy store owner is the target of a harassment campaign, the team goes undercover at a telephone exchange to nab the culprit. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dying man asks the team to clear the name of his son, a talented junior golfer who committed suicide after being implicated in the murder of a 10-year-old boy. Aiding the investigation is a former chief superintendent who admits she always had doubts about the teenager's guilt, but the committee members of the exclusive golf club where the victim's body was discovered prove reluctant to help the officers with their inquiries. Isla Blair and Cherie Lunghi guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An al-Qaeda terrorist leader offers to exchange intelligence on a planned bombing campaign in London for the pardoning of two prisoners, but the team faces a difficult choice when it becomes clear that defusing the explosives would endanger Lucas's role as a double agent. Harry learns the identity of the traitor who passed information to the Russians. Drama, starring Peter Firth and Richard Armitage

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June and Gina become convinced of Heaton's involvement in the property development conspiracy when he takes charge, announcing he will deal with Georgia in his own way. Evictions begin on the Aldbourne estate and Nikki faces a protest which escalates into a riot when fire bombs are thrown. A familiar face turns up at Sun Hill to see June clock off for the last time

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William are disappointed to learn Emma and Peter have made arrangements to be elsewhere over the festive period, so decide to treat their new French friends and neighbours to a typically English Christmas. Seasonal special from 1990, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing 12th-century monk investigates a murder as the Abbey becomes involved in the war between King Stephen and the Empress Maud. The trail of clues leads him to a cache of hidden treasure and the fleeing daughter of the castle's owner, Godith. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A friend lends the Crabbes his London flat for a weekend and promptly disappears, presenting them with a fresh mystery to solve. Meanwhile, Gary, Sally and a couple of agency staff make a meal of things back at the restaurant. Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed star

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe loses his sense of smell - and begins to have doubts about his future in the police. Meanwhile, Fisher is preoccupied by the prospect of a royal visit. Starring Richard Griffiths and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: General Wellesley receives unwanted reinforcements from the King of Spain in the form of the Royal Irish Company and Sharpe is charged with transforming them into an effective fighting unit. When Kiely learns his wife is pregnant, he sends her away out of harm's way, but she is captured. With Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Alex investigates a burglary and assault at the home of her future in-laws - a case that brings her face to face with 14-year-old Peter Drake, who will grow up to be her husband and Molly's father. Gene's initial lack of interest in the case ends when the fingerprints of a supposedly dead gangster are found at the scene, and he seizes the opportunity to get more positive headlines for the Met

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A murder victim is discovered in a canal, and Gene soon has his prime suspect when it turns out the dead man was mixed up with loan shark Trevor Riley. In their search for incriminating evidence, Gene and the team resort to breaking into Riley's office after hours - but what they find takes the case in a completely different direction. Guest starring Tom Georgeson and Daisy Haggard

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The detective looks into the suspicious death of an old friend and informant. The investigation leads him to the local police station, where it becomes clear the officers are hiding something - and to complicate matters, the disappearance of a teenage boy is also linked to the case. Crime drama, with Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and guest stars Neil Pearson, Dean Lennox Kelly and Mark Benton

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd is sent to the New Zealand town of Brokenwood to investigate the death of a farmer, after his body is found by two local fishermen. As he uncovers a family's tragedies and secrets, he also learns that Brokenwood is a place where shadows lurk just beneath the surface. Drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: A teenager is murdered at a halfway house for pregnant and wayward girls, and Phryne and Jack realise the root of the crime leads closer to home than they imagined possible. Australian drama set in the 1920s, starring Essie Davis and Nathan Page

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a prostitute in a high-class brothel. He identifies two possible suspects who both frequented the establishment, a well-known artist and a prominent judge. Meanwhile, the sleuth finds himself growing ever closer to Julia. Yannick Bisson and Helene Joy star

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A young woman's body is recovered from the bottom of a creek, and it transpires she had recently had an abortion - an illegal operation in 1890s Toronto. Murdoch and Julia's fledgling relationship is placed under strain by an argument concerning termination, and the detective nearly loses his objectivity while seeking those responsible for the victim's death. Crime drama, with Yannick Bisson and Helene Joy

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The discovery of a charred corpse in a disused railway tunnel sets Taggart on the gruesome hunt for a ruthless killer in an investigation with sinister undertones of black magic and voodoo. In an another twist, the wife of an arthritic dentist narrowly escapes a series of murder attempts, adding to a complex case for Taggart and his team. Starring Mark McManus, Blythe Duff and James MacPherson, and featuring a guest appearance by Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A friend lends the Crabbes his London flat for a weekend and promptly disappears, presenting them with a fresh mystery to solve. Meanwhile, Gary, Sally and a couple of agency staff make a meal of things back at the restaurant. Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy's wish for a quiet birthday is soon forgotten when Kezia announces she's got a party planned and he finds himself dodging bullets on the Aldbourne estate. Newly promoted DI Sam Nixon is put firmly in her place by Neil and promptly goes behind his back to revive the search for missing youngster Amy Tennant on the anniversary of her disappearance. June tells Tony and Reg she's taking early retirement

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sam's initiative on the anniversary of Amy Tennant's disappearance gives the team a lead when a friend of the family is seen stealing something from the missing child's bedroom. Emma and Diane investigate an abandoned van and unwittingly set loose a flock of frightened sheep, which turn out to be stolen stock on the way to an illegal slaughterhouse

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Emma works out that abductor Shelley Fitzgerald is likely to take Amy Tennant to her childhood haunts in Brighton. Lewis is the first to congratulate her for getting the hunt back on track, but husband Matt continues to belittle everything she does. Neil leads the team in a frantic search on the Sussex coast, where an elated James Tennant finally sets eyes on his daughter for the first time in a year

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Just two days away from retirement, June uncovers a swindle involving property developer Brian Stevens (guest star Mike Reid) and a local authority official involved in the sell-off of the Aldbourne estate. The trail leads close to home and, when DAC Georgia Hobbs is seen kissing John Heaton, June is left not knowing who she can trust

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Round Tower
programme.desc: Feature-length drama set in the 1950s, starring Emilia Fox as the rebellious teenage daughter of a successful businessman and his devoted wife, whose disenchantment with her privileged upbringing ultimately provokes a scandal in their small town. Keith Barron and Jan Harvey also star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: The army prepares to make another attack on the barricades as the fighting grows more vicious and desperate. Valjean feels conflicted about Marius' relationship with Cosette and faces a heartbreaking choice when he comes to realise what is in her best interests. However, he underestimates the strength of Cosette's feelings for him. Javert is thrown into turmoil by the selfless actions of a man he has always viewed as an irredeemable criminal, and is forced to reconsider everything he believes in

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Back on the murder squad, Pat is asked to investigate when a young mother of two, Diane Leach, is abducted from a local wood. The confession of a local sex offender complicates matters, as Pat looks into the possibility that the father of a young protege of the woman may be responsible for her disappearance. Meanwhile, Walker has worries of his own, as his ex-wife Lyn is dating an abusive, unstable man, who turns into a manic-obsessive when she decides to leave him

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah leads the charge to rescue James, whose ordeal continues when his sexuality becomes known, and puts her relationship with George at risk when a disturbing discovery forces her to reveal her darkest secret

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed

programme.title: Night Force

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion trawls the programme's archives and picks out some of the best Victorian antiques bought by the reds and blue teams over the years. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Southwell Racecourse with a mysterious Victorian glass object and Charlie Ross finds out just how seriously the Victorians took crime and punishment on a visit to Anglesey

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The makers of an acne-treatment product, a family card game and a sport support top designed to prevent injury during exercise watch on while their items are consumer-tested. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert Jay Blades uses his salvaging expertise at Altrincham Recycling Centre to search for three items that he can clean up and reinvent. Blacksmith Kev Paxton takes on the challenge of a metal window box in need of a complete makeover, while design team Josh Kennard and Oli Milne are challenged with finding a new use for some stepladders. Jay sees the value in some old tiles - but will he be able to turn all three items into money makers? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst are in Wales for their last leg, and both set out with bags of cash from their previous successes. Tim splashes out on some fine silver, while Phil risks a hernia by carrying a grandfather clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Mick needs to talk, so he calls Katy and confides that seeing her has taken him back to his childhood feeling of being lost and confused. Opening up, he confesses how miserable he has become. Meanwhile, Jack is not happy when he realises he has been played

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games. The host's cross-examination of the celebs over the course of five weeks provides an entertaining watch as the level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand hosts the quiz, inviting US comedian Reginald D Hunter and journalist Charlene White to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Denise has a hangover, but claims it is migraine, brought on by stress from the forthcoming wedding. Jim feels depressed by the rest of the family's insistence on ignoring his wishes and celebrating his birthday, so it is cut-price cakes, family photos and bubbly all round

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens' Drag Race UK tour arrives in London, where for the first time, Crystal is given the go-ahead to perform a risky aerial routine high above the stage. There is no safety rope, no net and no second chances

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: On their first night off from the tour, the queens hit a local drag show. Vinegar finds some action, while others cut out early like Divina - whose worsening flu continues to plague her

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Paul A Young, and special guest Jamie Cullum. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool and Manchester City v Burnley in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the opening last-64 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Ding Junhui is the reigning champion and he prevailed 6-2 over Michael Georgiou at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, Championship, FA Cup and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osmana a special food edition of the general knowledge quiz. This time, the contestants are Brian Turner, Stacie Stewart, Chris Bavin, Sabrina Ghayour, Jay Rayner, Loyd Grossman, Rick Stein and Jack Stein

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Iain and Olivia from Nottingham, sisters Melisa and Kaya from London and from Bridgend in Wales partners Krys and Shane

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the couples - minus Caroline and Johanes, who were eliminated last week - return to the Strictly ballroom with a new routine, hoping to secure their place in next week's musicals special

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. In each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund becomes jealous of the adulation that a boastful Sir Walter Raleigh has been receiving in court since his return from the New World. Attempting to impress the queen, he announces his intention to go on a voyage around the Cape of Good Hope, but his choice of captain leaves a lot to be desired. Comedy, guest starring Tom Baker and Simon Jones, with Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool at Amex Stadium and Manchester City v Burnley at Etihad Stadium. Liverpool and the Citizens have dominated their respective fixtures in recent years, but a number of unpredictable results this season so far demonstrated the need to avoid complacency

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including a triple bill of Thanksgiving Day contests - Detroit Lions v Houston Texans, Dallas Cowboys v Washington Football Team and Pittsburgh Steelers v Baltimore Ravens

programme.title: Westworld (1973)
programme.desc: At a huge theme park, guests can act out their fantasies in the company of lifelike robots. However, one tourist is left fighting for survival after the mechanoids begin to malfunction, and a sinister, seemingly unstoppable gunslinger sets its sights on killing him. Sci-fi thriller, starring Yul Brynner, Richard Benjamin and James Brolin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool at Amex Stadium and Manchester City v Burnley at Etihad Stadium. Liverpool and the Citizens have dominated their respective fixtures in recent years, but a number of unpredictable results this season so far demonstrated the need to avoid complacency

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Dr Paul Shackerley leads a service for the first Sunday of Advent from Brecon Cathedral. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet the new owners of a third-floor flat in Walworth, central London, and a block of flats in Hengoed in South Wales

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double edition of the antiques hunting show from the East of England Showground in Peterborough, and finds time to visit Blakesley Hall in Birmingham

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones hosts the boys' semi-final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year in Manchester, with five youngsters hoping to impress judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents highlights, analysis and talking points from the second round of the FA Cup

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, and have a budget of £400,000 to find their ideal home

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide which pair will leave the contest. Plus, a performance by Gary Barlow

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: In search of the knife, Will and Lyra try to gain entry into the Torre Degli Angeli, and discover a tied-up Giacomo. In Oxford, Boreal's offer of private funding is refused by Mary, while Lee finds Grumman - or Jopari as he is now known - and they take to the skies in search of the bearer. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Black forensic scientist Leroy Logan decides to join the Metropolitan Police in the hope of changing racist attitudes in the force from within its ranks. Leroy has to not only face the consequences of his father's disapproval, but also the blatant racism he finds in his new role as a despised yet exemplary constable in the Met. Fact-based drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, starring John Boyega and Steve Toussaint

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium and Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge. The Saints held United to a 1-1 draw in last season's corresponding fixture and will have fancied their changes of gaining at least a point once again given their strong start to the season, but most of United's best results this term have come on their travels. The London rivals will both have aspirations of challenging for the title and the Blues will also be hoping to avenge a penalty shootout defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Defending Digga D
programme.desc: Documentary following the 20-year-old drill artist after his release from a 15-month stint in prison for violent disorder, intent on making a success of his music career

programme.title: Coriolanus (2011)
programme.desc: A feared general is manipulated by his scheming mother into pursuing a political career, but his failure to win public approval results in riots and his banishment. Furious at the society that has cast him aside, he allies with an old enemy to get revenge. Shakespearean drama, directed by and starring Ralph Fiennes. With Gerard Butler and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast. Also in HD

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An exploration of insurance fraud, including dishonest claimants being caught on camera

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rummages around in more people's car boots, coming away from the recycling centre with an antique desk, two barley twist chairs and some grooming sets, hoping she and two other upcyclers can make a profit from them

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. Sue Nunn from Chichester is hoping wood restorer Will Kirk can bring her late husband's fishing tackle box back to its former glory. Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch have a huge task on their hands when they care for a doll with an extraordinary story. Margaret Dyson's beloved doll, Alice, was badly damaged in 1945 when an RAF plane crash landed into her family home, tragically killing her mother

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques valuer Caroline Hawley, trader Tony Wong and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to Skipton to find some bargains. Once the day is done they have to try and flip items - which include some horse kneepads - for a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Fiona confront Big Mac about his plan to change Weld. With Shay going back to school and staying with her father, Katie rents a tiny house-truck, and Poppy's mother comes to visit Woody and Tracey. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon present the art competition from Liverpool, and for the amateurs' first challenge they are asked to paint their personal interpretation of the city in watercolours. With just a week until the final, two artists make the bold decision to change their styles in a bid to impress the judges. For the quick draw, the hopefuls capture the Royal Liver Building clock tower, followed by the final challenge - a busy painting of the city's skyline from across the River Mersey

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon travel to Dartmouth for the final of the art competition. The four amateurs set up their easels at the Royal Naval College and are asked to capture a sense of Britain's seafaring heritage - and with just three challenges left, one contestant takes a big risk to impress the judges. That's followed by a quick sketch of a platoon of naval cadets marching on the parade ground, before the final painting of the competition, in which they have to capture the atmosphere of Dartmouth harbour. It's then up to judges Daphne Todd and Lachlan Goudie to decide who will be the first Big Painting Challenge winner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and bringing brand new valuations and unexpected auctions. Plus, presenter Paul Martin goes behind the scenes of Bamburgh Castle in Northumberland to see just what it takes to keep the building alive

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former footballer David James, stand-up comedian Rhys James, ex-track and field athlete Denise Lewis and comedian, actress, and writer Isy Suttie test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Analysis of the couples' progress as they prepare for tomorrow night's show. Gethin Jones is at Elstree Studios with the latest news from behind the scenes

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: As Storm Ophelia hits the British Isles, on the south east coast of Ireland the pagers of the Rosslare crew go off, to attend to a small yacht with three people on board that is caught in one of the worst storms in living memory. In Devon, the crew at Appledore race an incoming tide to try to reach a man stuck fast in quicksand, while on the north coast of Cornwall, the crew of St Agnes must battle through huge breaking waves to reach two young boys being swept out to sea in a deadly rip current

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Tranmere Rovers v Brackley Town (Kick-off 7.55pm). Coverage of the opening match in the second round of the competition at Prenton Park, with a potential money-spinning tie up for grabs in round three for the victors. Brackley, from the National League North, start as underdogs against the hosts, and needed a penalty shoot-out to defeat Bishop's Stortford in round one. Tranmere will be grateful for home advantage for the second round in succession, even without supporters allowed into the ground, having edged past Accrington Stanley 2-1 at the start of the month

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international news stories. With Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: There is celebration throughout the fleet when the Galactica finds the Battlestar Pegasus, a warship long thought destroyed by the Cylons with the rest of the colonial forces. However, tensions soon begin to surface as the new admiral tries to assert her authority and two of Adama's crew are sentenced to death

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. As the conflict between Galactica and Pegasus threatens to end in all-out war among the fleet, the Battlestars are forced to reunite to combat a new menace, a Cylon 'Resurrection' ship. Sharon struggles to put her attack behind her, while Adama and Roslin discover the fate of Admiral Cain's civilian passengers

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show clips from the natural world, including an elephant seal that turned up on a zebra crossing in Rio de Janeiro and a moose stuck up a tree

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: A sneak peek into the life of a monk in Laos

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield come to the aid of Carrie, a 10-year-old from Leeds who plans to celebrate her red hair with a gingerbread house den

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The countdown to Christmas starts soon so the presenters show how to make a Christmas tree advent calendar with some Strictly Come Dancing superstars. And Henry helps Mwaksy Mudende with her fear of dogs

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: New Zealand marine biologist Dr Ingrid Visser does not regard swimming with orcas to be dangerous, and does so regularly enough to know the local ones by sight. She sets out to investigate an unusually high number of deaths among the creatures in a year-long period between 2010 and 2011, and her findings reveal disturbing information about the state of the world's oceans

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the final, featuring the five heat winners and one of the runners-up. Judges Sethu Vijayakumar, Noel Sharkey and Dr Lucy Rodgers decide the winner, while Jonathan Pearce provides commentary on the mayhem

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Lyra the Pomsky moves in with the busy Cuthbertson family in Gateshead, but after the dog chews toddler Ellie's toys, her parent call in Louise to help stop the destruction and restore harmony to the house. In Cardiff, Ailsa has to decide whether she is confident enough to let Chinese crested powderpuff Fabio run free across the beach

programme.title: High Society (1956)
programme.desc: Romantic complications arise when a playboy composer sets out to win back his ex-wife. However, she is about to marry another man - and the situation becomes even more difficult when a cynical reporter enters the fray. Musical comedy based on The Philadelphia Story with a score by Cole Porter, starring Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Grace Kelly, with Louis Armstrong appearing as himself

programme.title: Swallows and Amazons (2016)
programme.desc: Four siblings on holiday in the Lake District discover an island, only to find another group of children have laid claim to it. The rivalry that ensues between the two factions must be put aside when a bigger and more dangerous challenge presents itself. Period drama based on Arthur Ransome's classic novel, starring Rafe Spall, Kelly Macdonald and Andrew Scott

programme.title: Britain's Lost Waterlands: Escape to Swallows and Amazons Country
programme.desc: Presenters Dick Strawbridge and Professor Alice Roberts explore the British landscapes that inspired children's author Arthur Ransome to write his famous series Swallows and Amazons. Strawbridge uses vintage boats to investigate three iconic waterlands - the Lake District, the Norfolk broads, and the Suffolk coastline - while Roberts takes a look at the shore lines

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Michael Baggot search for antiques at Manchester's Museum of Science and Industry. Paul Martin explores the city's musical history

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents further coverage of the last-64 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. The tournament moves away from its usual home of the Barbican Centre in York for the year to a venue that has staged the majority of tournaments during the Covid-19 pandemic. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Super League
programme.desc: Action from the Grand Final at the KCOM Stadium in Hull, as the two remaining sides battled it out for the title won by St Helens last season

programme.title: Nativity! (2009)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher is given the job of directing a nativity play and is determined to outdo a rival from a nearby private school. When he dishonestly claims his ex-girlfriend is a Hollywood producer and is coming to see the show with an eye on making a film, he finds himself becoming a local celebrity. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman and Ashley Jensen

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough tells the story of two physically identical species of ant that live in the same area in the foothills of the Swiss Alps, but have two very different strategies to survive the harsh environment

programme.title: Earth: One Amazing Day (2017)
programme.desc: Feature-length wildlife documentary from the same team the made David Attenborough's award-winning Planet Earth and Blue Planet documentaries, charting the story of life on Earth over the course of a single day. The film introduces viewers to a cast of characters including a baby zebra desperate to cross a swollen river, a penguin heroically undertaking a death-defying daily commute to feed its family, a family of sperm whales that like to snooze vertically and a sloth on the lookout for love. Narrated by Robert Redford

programme.title: Monsoon (2019)
programme.desc: A British-Vietnamese man returns to his birth country for the first time in more than 30 years. He was only eight when he and his family escaped Saigon as boat refugees during the Vietnam-American war. Now he embarks on a personal journey from Saigon to Hanoi to find a place to scatter his parents' ashes. Drama, starring Henry Golding, Parker Sawyers, David Tran and Molly Harris

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya presents action from the day's last-64 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents extended highlights of one of the day's best last-64 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter U, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley are in the Lincolnshire wolds at historic South Ormsby, which has ambitions to be the finest farming estate in the world. They discover the entrepreneurial spirit driving the revival of the local rural economy, including two young women carving out new careers. Elsewhere, Adam Henson has high hopes for an inner-city teenage jockey competing in her first race, while Tom Heap investigates whether it's time to ditch the green belt

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden visits Essex, where she experiments with triangular planting and has tea and cake at Beth Chatto Gardens. Plus, at RHS Hyde Hall she moves rocks with a former student, and then sails across the Thames to the Blackwater Estuary

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: During March and April, the horticulturist clears the detritus left behind by winter, as celandines, violets and primroses begin to bloom. She plants the first seeds of the year and is joined by a local gardener to prune the box hedge, but their work is interrupted by a nesting sparrow

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith heads to the west coast, investigating the risks of locating open water salmon farms in more exposed sites. Dougie Vipond is in Angus at the Montrose Basin, an important nature reserve, to help monitor its wintering birds. Anne Lundon follows the story of the green and white Scotways signs that point out rights of access routes across the Scottish countryside, while journalist Lesley Riddoch discusses her passion for hutting

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Like many people, Beechgrove's Kirsty Wilson is unable to afford a house with a garden. She shows how to grow stylishly and be green in a small home, from hangers to houseplants

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British pork, preparing a traditional gala pie and a rolled loin with apricots and ginger. They also explore the role the pig has played in the UK's history and travel to a farm in Gloucestershire to visit a couple raising rare breeds

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The dung beetle, an insect revered by ancient Egyptians, uses the sun, the moon and even the Milky Way to move its prized ball of dung in the right direction. Pigeons are often considered feeble birdbrains, but they have incredible memories that can recall several complex travel routes with amazing accuracy and they even use man-made roads and hedgerows to find the quickest way home. Here, the veteran broadcaster profiles animals with extraordinary abilities for finding the way

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the latest last-64 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Top seed Ronnie O'Sullivan was in dominant form at this stage last year, overcoming Tian Pengfei 6-0. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Lewis are at Herstmonceux Castle in East Sussex, where a Chinese wall plaque catches James's eye, and Paul Martin explores the history of the building

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents highlights of the second-round ties in the world's oldest domestic cup football competition, including Marine v Havant & Waterlooville, Shrewsbury Town v Oxford City and Bristol Rovers v Darlington

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of more business ideas. Up for consideration are a mother and son's range of spicy street food, a range of cocktails, and a former teacher's plan for a new system to mark students' schoolwork. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: The Trials of Oscar Pistorius
programme.desc: Six weeks into the trial, the defendant takes the stand, but his unconvincing testimony in the face of tough questioning leads to the charge being increased from culpable homicide to murder following an appeal. When a guilty verdict is returned, further appeals lead to his sentence rising from five year to 15

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up comedian Dane Baptiste plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by New York comic Desiree Burch and Liverpool funnyman Chris McCausland

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya presents action from the day's last-64 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of one of the day's best last-64 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max wrestles with the after-effects of neurosurgery and the financial pressure on the hospital, but things get worse when an encounter with an old enemy puts a strain on her relationship with Louis. Kian brings in some new recruits to help solve Jac's medical mystery, and a new arrival stirs up unpleasant memories for Donna

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites actor and comedian Ben Miller, broadcaster Grace Dent, comedian Desiree Burch and presenter Graham Norton to share their favourite books, reveal the books they pretend to have read but haven't and the book they would give someone on a first date. The panellists also discuss this week's book group pick, The Devil and the Dark Water by Stuart Turton, as well as Graham Norton's latest novel, Home Stretch

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: The singer and actress discusses how her Catholic faith has helped her overcome obstacles in her life, and how she suffered from anxiety at the height of her fame

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on April 12, with performances by artists including Jesus Jones, Technotronic featuring MC, Janet Jackson, Heart, Bizz Nizz, Paula Abdul, the Cure, Madonna and the Quireboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on April 19, with performances by artists including Sonia, the Quireboys, Adamski, the Blues Brothers, Alannah Myles, the Adventures of Stevie V, Faith No More, the Family Stand and Madonna

programme.title: Rod Stewart: Reel Stories
programme.desc: The singer talks to Dermot O'Leary about his life. From Abbey Road to the Royal Albert Hall and Motown to Maggie May, Dermot takes Rod back to his childhood cinema to relive moments from a career spanning six decades

programme.title: Gerry Rafferty: Right Down the Line
programme.desc: A profile of the singer-songwriter, who died in January 2011 and is best remembered for solo hits including Baker Street and Get It Right Next Time, and Stuck in the Middle with You by Stealers Wheel. The programme traces his life through his often autobiographical songs and features contributions by his daughter Martha, brother Jim and friends and colleagues including Billy Connolly, John Byrne and Joe Egan. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: The Proclaimers: This Is the Story
programme.desc: Superfan David Tennant meets Craig and Charlie Reid of Scottish band the Proclaimers, as they discuss the 30 years since their debut album, and a career that has taken them from playing in small pubs and clubs around Scotland to become one of the nation's most iconic bands

programme.title: Annie Lennox: BBC One Sessions
programme.desc: The Academy Award-winning singer performs at LSO St Luke's, London, with members of the BBC Concert Orchestra. The session includes songs from the Eurythmics' back catalogue, together with material from her solo career. First shown in 2009

programme.title: Travis with the BBC SSO at the Barrowland
programme.desc: Rock band Travis join forces with the 60-piece BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra at Glasgow's Barrowland Ballroom for a collaboration arranged for BBC Music Day in June 2016. Songs performed in front of the 1,000 audience members include All I Want to Do Is Rock, Sing and Why Does It Always Rain on Me?

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: A performance by the Brit Award-winning artist at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, recorded in 2018. The multiple Brit Award-winning singer and songwriter established herself as one of the UK's most exciting artists in 2012, with the release of her debut album Our Version of Events. The album spent a total of 10 weeks at number one and was the bestselling album of 2012 in the UK. Her follow-up Long Live the Angels came in 2016 and featured the singles Hurts and Breathing Underwater

programme.title: The Proclaimers: This Is the Story
programme.desc: Superfan David Tennant meets Craig and Charlie Reid of Scottish band the Proclaimers, as they discuss the 30 years since their debut album, and a career that has taken them from playing in small pubs and clubs around Scotland to become one of the nation's most iconic bands

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto visits Rome, the hilltop town of Tivoli and Naples, where he explores the city's subterranean tunnels and dodges its notoriously dangerous local motorists. He also stops off at the Galleria Borghese, and the water gardens at Villa d'Este, before meeting a family whose members live near the steaming sulphur jets of Solfatara

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The veteran traveller canoes through the remotest reaches of the Peruvian rainforest and finds the newly discovered Machigenga tribe have already caught on to plastic utensils and football boots. A more leisurely steamer trip takes him down the Amazon and on to Bogota, the drug-riddled crime capital of Colombia, where he shares lunch with a Godfather figure

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found murdered at the abandoned boys' home, and her body has similar wounds to those found in the previous two killings. Kata and Arnar are under pressure to identify and capture the killer. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata, Arnar and the Reykjavik team examine boxes of documents from an inquiry into Icelandic children's homes in an effort to identify both the next possible victim and the killer. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: Operation Iceberg
programme.desc: Chris Packham leads an expedition of adventurers and scientists to the Arctic to explore the unknown world of icebergs, following the frozen behemoths from their creation to their end. In the first of two programmes, the naturalist is joined by ocean specialist Helen Czerski, diver Andy Torbet and team medic Chris van Tulleken on a visit to Store Glacier in Greenland, to find out why it makes so many icebergs on such a huge scale. They look at the forces at work in this unpredictable environment, explore the inside of the glacier and witness the moment when a multi-million-ton block of ice breaks away to form an iceberg

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Taxi driver Dan Brolund, who was the last person to see Margrethe Thormod alive, turns out to also have connections to another victim. He is brought in for questioning, but at the same time suspicions about the Sonning couple grow. Saga feels she has come to a dead end investigating Henrik's missing children, and he slowly tries to face facts. Saga continues to attend therapy and after being given an assignment, realises that Henrik is the only person left in her life who means something to her. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Henrik thinks he has found the connection between all the victims, but when he adds the suspects to the investigation board he discovers a completely unexpected, but well-known face. Saga tries to explain her actions to Henrik, admitting that she did what she did because she thinks that she is in love with him - but he fails to understand. Noticing the frosty atmosphere between them, Lillian questions if they can continue to work together. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The veteran traveller canoes through the remotest reaches of the Peruvian rainforest and finds the newly discovered Machigenga tribe have already caught on to plastic utensils and football boots. A more leisurely steamer trip takes him down the Amazon and on to Bogota, the drug-riddled crime capital of Colombia, where he shares lunch with a Godfather figure

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto visits Rome, the hilltop town of Tivoli and Naples, where he explores the city's subterranean tunnels and dodges its notoriously dangerous local motorists. He also stops off at the Galleria Borghese, and the water gardens at Villa d'Este, before meeting a family whose members live near the steaming sulphur jets of Solfatara

programme.title: Crossing England in a Punt: River of Dreams
programme.desc: Tom Fort embarks on a 170-mile journey down the River Trent in his own custom-built punt. The presenter encounters the power stations that generate much of the nation's electricity, meets veterans of the floods of 1947 and learns about a Bronze Age boatman who made a life on the banks as he discovers the waterway's 3,000-year history

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: From enormous tortoises and deep-diving lizards to fish-eating snakes and birds that hunt giant, venomous centipedes, the wildlife of the Galapagos Islands is unique and bizarre. However, the wilderness that inspired Charles Darwin and the theory of evolution is becoming ever-more crowded due to increased migration and tourism. David Attenborough narrates this documentary, which examines how the Pacific archipelago's animals are adapting to their changing circumstances

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter reflects on three centuries of interaction and conflict between the Romans and ancient Scottish tribes. He examines whether the Scots were too strong for the invading army or if the country was simply considered not worth the effort of conquering, and reveals a story of a superpower pitted against tribesmen and warlords, and one with fascinating modern parallels

programme.title: Balloon (2018)
programme.desc: Thuringen, East Germany in 1979, and like many of the country's citizens, the Strelzyk family are desperate to escape to the west. Under the watchful eye of the Stasi and the fearsome lieutenant colonel Seidel, they try to cross the border in a homemade hot air balloon. Drama, starring Friedrich Mucke and Alicia von Rittberg. In German with English subtitles

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990

But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran join Jaxon and Ezra in the park for Cynthia’s pirate-themed treasure hunt. Who will be the first to find the treasure? Come on, shipmates, and set sail with JoJo and Gran Gran to find out!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory Crawford isits Lily and her cat, Brody, and they clean out her litter tray and feed her together, before having lots of cuddles. Later, they head to the cat grooming parlour

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, Grace shows off three amazing machines that help with farming! Grace drives a massive compost turner, shows off a machine that makes hay bales and then picks some tasty carrots in a carrot harvester!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups with leaves and enjoy songs and story time together.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: African folk tale explaining why buffalo have horns

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: A wrong bus later and the pair are back where they started! It's time to look at the schedule and see when their bus is due to arrive.

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie and Liam are looking for a large piece of paper to do their next big painting on, but when their parents are too busy in the Post Office to help and Grannie Island is unable to assist, the feisty seven-year-old calls at the Lady Artist's house in the hope she will know where to get one from

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Bug and his friends lead the duo to their outdoor music concert. The adventures of a seven-year-old girl and her duck

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: An imaginary friends comes to Lola's aid when she has no one else to play with

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo can make Tee crack-up laughing with one tickle, but Tee is unable to get her to laugh in return. Tee gets more and more frustrated as he tries to find Mo's tickle spot

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The creatures are visited by a rare, strange and beautiful creature a Moon Whale. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka loses his sponge while cleaning stones

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Tom Hardy tells the tale of Felipe the cactus, who is in search of something we all need: a hug.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Property series in which people get the chance to experience life in Australia or New Zealand before deciding whether they want to make the move permanently

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A gang of fraudsters' million-pound wine bar insurance racket is uncovered, and insurers receive a bizarre claim after a dog damages an expensive surgical bed
