programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The makers of an acne-treatment product, a family card game and a sport support top designed to prevent injury during exercise watch on while their items are consumer-tested. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley travels to Somerset to help a couple with a £450,000 budget find their very own patch of greenery after 18 years without a garden. Whilst in the county, Ginny visits the Rural Life Museum to join the medieval multimillion-pound refurbishment project

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert Jay Blades uses his salvaging expertise at Altrincham Recycling Centre to search for three items that he can clean up and reinvent. Blacksmith Kev Paxton takes on the challenge of a metal window box in need of a complete makeover, while design team Josh Kennard and Oli Milne are challenged with finding a new use for some stepladders. Jay sees the value in some old tiles - but will he be able to turn all three items into money makers? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst are in Wales for their last leg, and both set out with bags of cash from their previous successes. Tim splashes out on some fine silver, while Phil risks a hernia by carrying a grandfather clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Mick needs to talk, so he calls Katy and confides that seeing her has taken him back to his childhood feeling of being lost and confused. Opening up, he confesses how miserable he has become. Meanwhile, Jack is not happy when he realises he has been played

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games. The host's cross-examination of the celebs over the course of five weeks provides an entertaining watch as the level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand hosts the quiz, inviting US comedian Reginald D Hunter and journalist Charlene White to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod works at the UK's largest open cast mine in Merthyr Tydfil, where he is tested on everything from driving a 25-ton truck to handling. Moving onto Europe's deepest mine, Rhod has to learn to work nine kilometres under the North Sea with a foreman who has no time for his whingeing

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old schoolgirl Muriel Drinkwater was murdered in 1946, brutally assaulted in the woods near her home in Penllergaer, before being shot. Over the decades, although the culprit has never been caught, several key discoveries have been made to advance the investigation. But due in part to the scientific limitations of the day, such discoveries failed to produce major breakthroughs. Here, the team applies modern-day forensic techniques to the available facts. What investigators uncover edges Muriel's killer closer to being identified and provides answers to at least some of the questions that her family has been asking since the day she died

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens' Drag Race UK tour arrives in London, where for the first time, Crystal is given the go-ahead to perform a risky aerial routine high above the stage. There is no safety rope, no net and no second chances

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: On their first night off from the tour, the queens hit a local drag show. Vinegar finds some action, while others cut out early like Divina - whose worsening flu continues to plague her

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Paul A Young, and special guest Jamie Cullum. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool and Manchester City v Burnley in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the opening last-64 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Ding Junhui is the reigning champion and he prevailed 6-2 over Michael Georgiou at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, Championship, FA Cup and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osmana a special food edition of the general knowledge quiz. This time, the contestants are Brian Turner, Stacie Stewart, Chris Bavin, Sabrina Ghayour, Jay Rayner, Loyd Grossman, Rick Stein and Jack Stein

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Iain and Olivia from Nottingham, sisters Melisa and Kaya from London and from Bridgend in Wales partners Krys and Shane

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the couples - minus Caroline and Johanes, who were eliminated last week - return to the Strictly ballroom with a new routine, hoping to secure their place in next week's musicals special

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. In each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund becomes jealous of the adulation that a boastful Sir Walter Raleigh has been receiving in court since his return from the New World. Attempting to impress the queen, he announces his intention to go on a voyage around the Cape of Good Hope, but his choice of captain leaves a lot to be desired. Comedy, guest starring Tom Baker and Simon Jones, with Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool at Amex Stadium and Manchester City v Burnley at Etihad Stadium. Liverpool and the Citizens have dominated their respective fixtures in recent years, but a number of unpredictable results this season so far demonstrated the need to avoid complacency

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including a triple bill of Thanksgiving Day contests - Detroit Lions v Houston Texans, Dallas Cowboys v Washington Football Team and Pittsburgh Steelers v Baltimore Ravens

programme.title: Westworld (1973)
programme.desc: At a huge theme park, guests can act out their fantasies in the company of lifelike robots. However, one tourist is left fighting for survival after the mechanoids begin to malfunction, and a sinister, seemingly unstoppable gunslinger sets its sights on killing him. Sci-fi thriller, starring Yul Brynner, Richard Benjamin and James Brolin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Dr Paul Shackerley leads a service for the first Sunday of Advent from Brecon Cathedral. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet the new owners of a third-floor flat in Walworth, central London, and a block of flats in Hengoed in South Wales

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double edition of the antiques hunting show from the East of England Showground in Peterborough, and finds time to visit Blakesley Hall in Birmingham

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones hosts the boys' semi-final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year in Manchester, with five youngsters hoping to impress judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents highlights, analysis and talking points from the second round of the FA Cup

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, and have a budget of £400,000 to find their ideal home

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen looks at the gender divide in Wales, featuring archive clips of ladies' nights, working men's clubs and competitive darts

programme.title: Wales and the History of the World
programme.desc: Eddie Butler continues his exploration of the stories behind objects in Welsh museums, focusing on the country's contribution on international trade and industry, from the woollen sleeping bags for the Russian army, to the tinplate that kept food fresh for the pioneers of the Wild West

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide which pair will leave the contest. Plus, a performance by Gary Barlow

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: In search of the knife, Will and Lyra try to gain entry into the Torre Degli Angeli, and discover a tied-up Giacomo. In Oxford, Boreal's offer of private funding is refused by Mary, while Lee finds Grumman - or Jopari as he is now known - and they take to the skies in search of the bearer. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Black forensic scientist Leroy Logan decides to join the Metropolitan Police in the hope of changing racist attitudes in the force from within its ranks. Leroy has to not only face the consequences of his father's disapproval, but also the blatant racism he finds in his new role as a despised yet exemplary constable in the Met. Fact-based drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, starring John Boyega and Steve Toussaint

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: George Shotton wed Mamie Stuart in 1918, despite already being a married man. Though suspected of Mamie's disappearance a year later, he evaded conviction. It wasn't until 1961, when divers discovered human remains in a cave near Caswell Bay, that he was posthumously sentenced. The team looks at a 1934 incident known as the Brighton Trunk Murder, in which a dismembered body was found split between two trunks. Given the similarities between this murder and that of Mamie Stuart, the team believes that Shotton could be a prime suspect for this murder too

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, following the lives of a working-class Manchester family and set almost exclusively in their living room. With Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Another leading performer on the stand-up circuit plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by fellow comedians ready to share their light-hearted views on modern life

programme.title: Coriolanus (2011)
programme.desc: A feared general is manipulated by his scheming mother into pursuing a political career, but his failure to win public approval results in riots and his banishment. Furious at the society that has cast him aside, he allies with an old enemy to get revenge. Shakespearean drama, directed by and starring Ralph Fiennes. With Gerard Butler and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast. Also in HD

programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith heads to the west coast, investigating the risks of locating open water salmon farms in more exposed sites. programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents highlights, analysis and talking points from the second round of the FA Cup

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, and have a budget of £400,000 to find their ideal home

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide which pair will leave the contest. Plus, a performance by Gary Barlow

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: In search of the knife, Will and Lyra try to gain entry into the Torre Degli Angeli, and discover a tied-up Giacomo. In Oxford, Boreal's offer of private funding is refused by Mary, while Lee finds Grumman - or Jopari as he is now known - and they take to the skies in search of the bearer. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Black forensic scientist Leroy Logan decides to join the Metropolitan Police in the hope of changing racist attitudes in the force from within its ranks. Leroy has to not only face the consequences of his father's disapproval, but also the blatant racism he finds in his new role as a despised yet exemplary constable in the Met. Fact-based drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, starring John Boyega and Steve Toussaint

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium and Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge. The Saints held United to a 1-1 draw in last season's corresponding fixture and will have fancied their changes of gaining at least a point once again given their strong start to the season, but most of United's best results this term have come on their travels. The London rivals will both have aspirations of challenging for the title and the Blues will also be hoping to avenge a penalty shootout defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Defending Digga D
programme.desc: Documentary following the 20-year-old drill artist after his release from a 15-month stint in prison for violent disorder, intent on making a success of his music career

programme.title: Coriolanus (2011)
programme.desc: A feared general is manipulated by his scheming mother into pursuing a political career, but his failure to win public approval results in riots and his banishment. Furious at the society that has cast him aside, he allies with an old enemy to get revenge. Shakespearean drama, directed by and starring Ralph Fiennes. With Gerard Butler and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast. Also in HD

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent, while Martin Roberts meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion Dublin looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme, Staffordshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A gang of fraudsters' million-pound wine bar insurance racket is uncovered, and insurers receive a bizarre claim after a dog damages an expensive surgical bed

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: A glimpse into the lives of the invisible army of workers driving lorries, making milk deliveries and cleaning streets at night. Plus, the radio DJ who keeps the insomniacs of Newcastle company

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion trawls the programme's archives and picks out some of the best Victorian antiques bought by the reds and blue teams over the years. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Southwell Racecourse with a mysterious Victorian glass object and Charlie Ross finds out just how seriously the Victorians took crime and punishment on a visit to Anglesey

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The makers of an acne-treatment product, a family card game and a sport support top designed to prevent injury during exercise watch on while their items are consumer-tested. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley travels to Somerset to help a couple with a £450,000 budget find their very own patch of greenery after 18 years without a garden. Whilst in the county, Ginny visits the Rural Life Museum to join the medieval multimillion-pound refurbishment project

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert Jay Blades uses his salvaging expertise at Altrincham Recycling Centre to search for three items that he can clean up and reinvent. Blacksmith Kev Paxton takes on the challenge of a metal window box in need of a complete makeover, while design team Josh Kennard and Oli Milne are challenged with finding a new use for some stepladders. Jay sees the value in some old tiles - but will he be able to turn all three items into money makers? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst are in Wales for their last leg, and both set out with bags of cash from their previous successes. Tim splashes out on some fine silver, while Phil risks a hernia by carrying a grandfather clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former swimmer Fran Halsall, ex-Everton footballer Leon Osman, three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby and Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Mick needs to talk, so he calls Katy and confides that seeing her has taken him back to his childhood feeling of being lost and confused. Opening up, he confesses how miserable he has become. Meanwhile, Jack is not happy when he realises he has been played

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games. The host's cross-examination of the celebs over the course of five weeks provides an entertaining watch as the level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand hosts the quiz, inviting US comedian Reginald D Hunter and journalist Charlene White to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they take a satirical sideswipe at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Denise has a hangover, but claims it is migraine, brought on by stress from the forthcoming wedding. Jim feels depressed by the rest of the family's insistence on ignoring his wishes and celebrating his birthday, so it is cut-price cakes, family photos and bubbly all round

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Tim McGarry, Colin Murphy, Jake O' Kane and Neil Delamere poke fun at the people and events that have been making the news in Northern Ireland. They are joined by comedian and actress Caroline Rhea, who makes contact from Los Angeles via the internet

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens' Drag Race UK tour arrives in London, where for the first time, Crystal is given the go-ahead to perform a risky aerial routine high above the stage. There is no safety rope, no net and no second chances

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: On their first night off from the tour, the queens hit a local drag show. Vinegar finds some action, while others cut out early like Divina - whose worsening flu continues to plague her

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Paul A Young, and special guest Jamie Cullum. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool and Manchester City v Burnley in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the opening last-64 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Ding Junhui is the reigning champion and he prevailed 6-2 over Michael Georgiou at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, Championship, FA Cup and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osmana a special food edition of the general knowledge quiz. This time, the contestants are Brian Turner, Stacie Stewart, Chris Bavin, Sabrina Ghayour, Jay Rayner, Loyd Grossman, Rick Stein and Jack Stein

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Iain and Olivia from Nottingham, sisters Melisa and Kaya from London and from Bridgend in Wales partners Krys and Shane

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the couples - minus Caroline and Johanes, who were eliminated last week - return to the Strictly ballroom with a new routine, hoping to secure their place in next week's musicals special

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. In each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund becomes jealous of the adulation that a boastful Sir Walter Raleigh has been receiving in court since his return from the New World. Attempting to impress the queen, he announces his intention to go on a voyage around the Cape of Good Hope, but his choice of captain leaves a lot to be desired. Comedy, guest starring Tom Baker and Simon Jones, with Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool at Amex Stadium and Manchester City v Burnley at Etihad Stadium. Liverpool and the Citizens have dominated their respective fixtures in recent years, but a number of unpredictable results this season so far demonstrated the need to avoid complacency

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including a triple bill of Thanksgiving Day contests - Detroit Lions v Houston Texans, Dallas Cowboys v Washington Football Team and Pittsburgh Steelers v Baltimore Ravens

programme.title: Westworld (1973)
programme.desc: At a huge theme park, guests can act out their fantasies in the company of lifelike robots. However, one tourist is left fighting for survival after the mechanoids begin to malfunction, and a sinister, seemingly unstoppable gunslinger sets its sights on killing him. Sci-fi thriller, starring Yul Brynner, Richard Benjamin and James Brolin

programme.title: 100 Women Extra
programme.desc: Finland has a coalition government led by five women - four of them are in their 30s. Their prime minister, Sanna Marin, was the youngest world leader when she was elected. Finland's coalition government has been praised for their quick and decisive action on the coronavirus pandemic, but a coalition can also mean compromise. The BBC has had world-first exclusive access behind the scenes to explore how the coalition works in practice, how representative it is of Finland's society, and what impact the age and sex of the government's main players has had on its policies and effectiveness.

programme.title: The Papers

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special, women-focused edition of Witness History, the BBC's Ciru Muriuki in Nairobi brings us five moments from the recent past as told to us by the women who were there. We hear from the daughter of the world's first female prime minister. We also learn about the fight for equal rights for women at the Western Wall in Jerusalem and the campaign for the right to take the contraceptive pill in Japan. Plus, Nasa's female space pioneers and one of the crew members of the Discovery Shuttle mission that put into orbit one of the most remarkable scientific instruments of all time, the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: To celebrate BBC 100 Women, Click profiles the most inspiring and influential women in technology, from the leaders shaping our future to those finding solutions to the challenges thrown up by the pandemic. The women featured are those driving change, not just for women, but for all parts of society. Hearing their inspirational stories, we learn not only how these women are making an impact, but also the challenges they've faced and the diverse backgrounds they come from. Using in-depth profiles, we provide audiences with a rare and intimate glimpse into the lives of the women who are transforming the world of technology at a unique time in our history. Women who are steering global technology policy, fighting bias in artificial intelligence, using data from sewers to combat Covid-19 and creating fertility solutions. Others have founded companies focusing on mental health, online abuse, women's health, vertical farming and current transport restrictions. With rare-access only Click can

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: To celebrate BBC 100 Women, Click profiles the most inspiring and influential women in technology, from the leaders shaping our future to those finding solutions to the challenges thrown up by the pandemic. The women featured are those driving change, not just for women, but for all parts of society. Hearing their inspirational stories, we learn not only how these women are making an impact, but also the challenges they've faced and the diverse backgrounds they come from. Using in-depth profiles, we provide audiences with a rare and intimate glimpse into the lives of the women who are transforming the world of technology at a unique time in our history. Women who are steering global technology policy, fighting bias in artificial intelligence, using data from sewers to combat Covid-19 and creating fertility solutions. Others have founded companies focusing on mental health, online abuse, women's health, vertical farming and current transport restrictions. With rare-access only Click can

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: 100 Women Extra
programme.desc: Finland has a coalition government led by five women - four of them are in their 30s. Their prime minister, Sanna Marin, was the youngest world leader when she was elected. Finland's coalition government has been praised for their quick and decisive action on the coronavirus pandemic, but a coalition can also mean compromise. The BBC has had world-first exclusive access behind the scenes to explore how the coalition works in practice, how representative it is of Finland's society, and what impact the age and sex of the government's main players has had on its policies and effectiveness.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special, women-focused edition of Witness History, the BBC's Ciru Muriuki in Nairobi brings us five moments from the recent past as told to us by the women who were there. We hear from the daughter of the world's first female prime minister. We also learn about the fight for equal rights for women at the Western Wall in Jerusalem and the campaign for the right to take the contraceptive pill in Japan. Plus, Nasa's female space pioneers and one of the crew members of the Discovery Shuttle mission that put into orbit one of the most remarkable scientific instruments of all time, the Hubble telescope.

programme.desc: Historian Martin MacGregor has had a life-long fascination with King Robert the Bruce. Enlisting the help of Professor Caroline Wilkinson, a leading expert in the field of forensic facial reconstruction, Martin comes face to face with his hero

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell returns to the Millennium Forum in Derry to present a special programme with the Quinn family. He and the audience get an insight into the lives of Brendan, James and Stephen Quinn, who hail from Magherafelt, Co Derry

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean swims off the beaches of Vatersay

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: A live set by The Futureheads recorded at Glasgow's QMU, and sessions from Tide Lines and Zoe Bestel. Niteworks drop in to talk about their life in music

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Erik lives alone in a run-down campervan on a desolate beach, while his twin Adam is the complete opposite - a successful man with money, a thriving family business, a loving wife and two children. When Erik's irresponsible lifestyle finally catches up with him, he contacts his brother for help, but Adam brutally rejects him - with fatal consequences. Norwegian crime thriller, starring Kristofer Hivju

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Music recorded in Edinburgh, Lewis and Belfast in 2015 and 2016, featuring the likes of Dónal Clancy, Alasdair White, Seán Mac Corraidh and Paul Meehan

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features. Plus, Luke Goss chats about starring in new film The Loss Adjuster

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Middlesbrough, where Karen Dalmeny is shaken but not stirred as she tries to buy a James Bond book, Tony Geering's eyes pop for some miniature glasses; and Jo Brayshaw scrambles some cash for an 'eggs-tra' special piece of porcelain

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Rory, Donna, Julie, Sharron, David, Steve, Jenny, Gill and Stu are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Moon, Glen, Storme and Ocean work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leanna intends to start a smear campaign on Gabby, Jai tells Ms Thomas about a job, and Nicola tries to listen in on some gossip

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne faces the toughest day of her life, a visit from Jenny's stepdaughter provides a welcome distraction, and Tim and Alya search Geoff's home while he's out

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Riding an historic Tiger Moth bi-plane, Griff Rhys Jones arrives in the humid tropics of Darwin. He encounters a crocodile and an iconic news editor, before travelling through the night on a road train, arriving in the 'red centre' of Alice Springs - only to be surprised by the lush green sporting fields and the mysterious Americans playing baseball there. The presenter gets some tips on how to survive in the desert from an Aboriginal elder, before riding into the sunset on a camel - in tribute to the Afghan cameleers for which the legendary Ghan passenger train is named

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne and Steve say a heartbreaking goodbye to Oliver. The police interrupt Johnny and Jenny's row, and arrest him on suspicion of burglary and GBH. Geoff orders Tim and Alya to leave his house

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Will Friday on day 13 be unlucky for some of the contestants? All will be revealed as Ant and Dec reflect on highlights from the past day, and look ahead to the weekend's events

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha joins forces with Queen of curries Nisha Katona for a family feast from India. The duo serve up tea-steeped chickpeas with black cardamom rice for breakfast, tardka dhal for lunch and Nisha's gingerbread lamb raan for dinner - with Nadia's carrot halwa for dessert

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is lost in a pyramid but he is in fact in a big conference room where Fizz is about to perform a stand-up act.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has dressed Weasel up as a supervillain in order to pretend to save the city. Magoo mistakes his disguised neighbour for an actress in danger.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel accidentally launches Fizz next to Magoo. The latter, believing he is driving through a wildlife reserve, mistakes Fizz for a lost child.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The team travels to Langstrom Fischler's newest invention to stop a hive of malfunctioning weather drones. With the voices of Rhys Darby, Rosamund Pike and Kayvan Novak

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's challenge will see him attempt to escape from a helicopter simulator underwater. Has he got what it takes to be submerged?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: A guilty conscience causes Butch to change his ways. But Tom, Jerry, Spike and Tuffy quickly decide they like the old Butch better

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Dracula gets an endorsement deal and he names the prototype after Mavis

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The Mystery Inc. team is back and just as courageous, adventurous, humorous, and obsessed with cases as ever. When a desperate Steve Urkel turns to the team for help on tracking down his Urkel-Bot in Chicago, they stumble onto a deeper mystery with the appearance of the Technomancer, who has the ability to control all robots.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Cliff Richard, while chefs Sat Bains and Richard Bertinet are on hand to provide a selection of recipes. James also provides a masterclass on chocolate mousse, and travels to the Scottish Highlands for his latest food adventure

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a three-course menu perfect for a romantic evening. For starters, there is pork rilette served with a garlic and cheese bread, while the main course is salmon en croute, with a side order of hasselback root vegetables, peas and beetroot and a fresh horseradish sauce. For dessert, Lisa prepares a marble chocolate and orange bundt cake with chocolate sauce

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef continues his American adventure in Austin, Texas. James starts with a breakfast taco at iconic Torchy's, followed by a cowboy themed shopping spree. He then cooks in front of a mural dedicated to his favourite ingredient - butter

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents live coverage of today's racing, including the 2.05 Fighting Fifth Hurdle from Newcastle and 3.00 Ladbrokes Trophy from Newbury. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Oli Bell, Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey and Natalie Green, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara host the penultimate edition of the obstacle-course contest, as competitors from across the UK test their strength, speed and courage. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Athlete Mo Farah competes in the game show, recorded shortly before he went on to win two gold medals in the London Olympic Games. Given nine lives at the start, he aims to complete up to seven fiendishly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, in the hope of winning up to £250,000 for his charity, the Mo Farah Foundation. Could this remarkable athlete be the first person to take away the jackpot? Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: Catchphrase: Catchiest Moments
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern presents a second compilation of some of his favourite moments from the quiz show, from the funniest answers to the strangest. Featuring previously unseen footage, and giving viewers a chance to play along at home as contestants make unexpected attempts to Say What They See

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents an I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here! special, as former campmates Iain Lee, Jennie McAlpine, Shappi Khorsandi and Stanley Johnson pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: After two weeks at the castle, the contestants have endured all manner of tasks and ordeals, but the end is in sight. As ever, Ant and Dec guide viewers through highlights from the past 24 hours, and look ahead to the next stage

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Matt Lucas, fresh from completing his first series as new host of The Great British Bake Off, as well as comedy power couple Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont, Dancing on Ice and Don't Hate The Playaz star Lady Leshurr and artist Grayson Perry, while Liam Gallagher performs in the studio

programme.title: Tyson Fury: The Gypsy King
programme.desc: The opening instalment in a three-part documentary following the boxer as he prepares for his rematch with Deontay Wilder for the WBC World Heavyweight title, after the first contest in December 2018 ended in a much-disputed draw. Filmed over several months, the programme provides an insight into the life of one of British sport's most flamboyant and controversial characters and those who are closest to him

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through to the quarter-finals, two families go head to head for a place in the semi-finals of the tournament, aiming to move a step closer to winning the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family and a holiday of a lifetime. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's underwater scooting and has discovered Atlantis. In fact, he's in New York, riding Fizz's latest invention.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are trying to steal the president's jokebook. Magoo, thinking he is visiting a museum of sorcery, mistakes Weasel for a wizard.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a hoverboard. After a slip-up, the board ends up in the hands of Magoo, who thinks he has found a new ironing board.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Scott, Virgil and Kayo race to save a plummeting cargo zeppelin, which is equipped with a loading claw and fuel source that could prove deadly should the vessel crash. With the voices of Rasmus Hardiker, David Menkin and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's in Blackpool but is he brave enough to walk to the top of the UK's tallest rollercoaster?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Hank discovers his thumb comes from a famed, Dracula ancestor

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Always getting picked on at school, Karen Beecher discovers that tiny Bumblebee might be the only one small enough to save the day.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Setting off from Rosthwaite, a small village in the jaws of Borrowdale, the expert walker takes in the history and landscape of the Cumbrian fells as she heads up the small peak Castle Crag, which stands at less than 1,000ft. The presenter is joined by celebrated mountaineer Chris Bonington, to whom she asks - which is better - Everest or Castle Crag? From the lush green valley floor to magnificent views over Derwent Water, this walk is a great way to take home a lasting memory of the Lake District, and all in under three hours

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh in Hampshire this week are Ronan Keating, who'll be trying the best British whiskies, and David Jason, who shares his remarkable life story.

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Alex Beresford and Ria Hebden visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Riding an historic Tiger Moth bi-plane, Griff Rhys Jones arrives in the humid tropics of Darwin. He encounters a crocodile and an iconic news editor, before travelling through the night on a road train, arriving in the 'red centre' of Alice Springs - only to be surprised by the lush green sporting fields and the mysterious Americans playing baseball there. The presenter gets some tips on how to survive in the desert from an Aboriginal elder, before riding into the sunset on a camel - in tribute to the Afghan cameleers for which the legendary Ghan passenger train is named

programme.title: Kindergarten Cop (1990)
programme.desc: A tough detective is forced to pose as a preschool teacher in a bid to capture a drug dealer whose son is one of the pupils. However, his biggest challenge turns out to be controlling a class full of five-year-olds. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Penelope Ann Miller, Linda Hunt, Pamela Reed and Richard Tyson

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. James, Rak, Lynn and Chris work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Lily, Siona, Mohammed, Tom, Sam, Stephen, Sharon, Judith and Danny use their wits and numbers to try to get through to the final to win the prize pot. Hosted by Omid Djalili

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Phillip Schofield, in which married couple Matt and Paige take on twin sisters Lauren and Danielle. They attempt to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in the hope of winning more than £50,000

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard welcomes funny man Richard Herring, actor Joe McFadden and reality star Olivia Attwood. They answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Who Wants To Be A Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: Former teacher David Edwards looks back on his life-changing journey to success, along with wife Viv and his phone a friend, son Richard. Plus, there's a look at the memorable games of celebrities including Lee Mack and Stacey Solomon

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: The celebrities have endured two weeks of castle life, and they continue to battle it out in the quest to be crowned winner of the 2020 contest. Ant and Dec present

programme.title: Tyson Fury: The Gypsy King
programme.desc: The heavyweight celebrates a victory over Tom Schwarz in Las Vegas by enjoying a night of heavy drinking back home in Morecambe and video-calling rival Anthony Joshua. This angers his wife Paris, who is reminded of his alcohol abuse during his years of depression, and Fury looks to make amends by treating the family to a day out in Blackpool and booking a family holiday. The boxer then heads to Marbella with trainer Ben Davison, before preparing to face his next opponent Otto Wallin back in Vegas, in what proves to be a challenging fight

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a selection of clips and profiles from the ITV Sport archives, including an interview with a young Tyson Fury predicting great things for his future in boxing.

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Action from Knockhill, featuring round eight of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and round 12 of the British F4 Championship

programme.desc: A man claims £300 owed on loans he made to his former girlfriend to help pay for rent, living expenses and a modelling portfolio

programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are trying to steal the president's jokebook. Magoo, thinking he is visiting a museum of sorcery, mistakes Weasel for a wizard.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a hoverboard. After a slip-up, the board ends up in the hands of Magoo, who thinks he has found a new ironing board.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Scott, Virgil and Kayo race to save a plummeting cargo zeppelin, which is equipped with a loading claw and fuel source that could prove deadly should the vessel crash. With the voices of Rasmus Hardiker, David Menkin and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's in Blackpool but is he brave enough to walk to the top of the UK's tallest rollercoaster?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Always getting picked on at school, Karen Beecher discovers that tiny Bumblebee might be the only one small enough to save the day.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Setting off from Rosthwaite, a small village in the jaws of Borrowdale, the expert walker takes in the history and landscape of the Cumbrian fells as she heads up the small peak Castle Crag, which stands at less than 1,000ft. The presenter is joined by celebrated mountaineer Chris Bonington, to whom she asks - which is better - Everest or Castle Crag? From the lush green valley floor to magnificent views over Derwent Water, this walk is a great way to take home a lasting memory of the Lake District, and all in under three hours

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh in Hampshire this week are Ronan Keating, who'll be trying the best British whiskies, and David Jason, who shares his remarkable life story.

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Alex Beresford and Ria Hebden visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Riding an historic Tiger Moth bi-plane, Griff Rhys Jones arrives in the humid tropics of Darwin. He encounters a crocodile and an iconic news editor, before travelling through the night on a road train, arriving in the 'red centre' of Alice Springs - only to be surprised by the lush green sporting fields and the mysterious Americans playing baseball there. The presenter gets some tips on how to survive in the desert from an Aboriginal elder, before riding into the sunset on a camel - in tribute to the Afghan cameleers for which the legendary Ghan passenger train is named

programme.title: Kindergarten Cop (1990)
programme.desc: A tough detective is forced to pose as a preschool teacher in a bid to capture a drug dealer whose son is one of the pupils. However, his biggest challenge turns out to be controlling a class full of five-year-olds. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Penelope Ann Miller, Linda Hunt, Pamela Reed and Richard Tyson

programme.title: The Chase

James, Rak, Lynn and Chris work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard welcomes funny man Richard Herring, actor Joe McFadden and reality star Olivia Attwood. They answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.desc: The heavyweight celebrates a victory over Tom Schwarz in Las Vegas by enjoying a night of heavy drinking back home in Morecambe and video-calling rival Anthony Joshua. This angers his wife Paris, who is reminded of his alcohol abuse during his years of depression, and Fury looks to make amends by treating the family to a day out in Blackpool and booking a family holiday. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The medic struggles to dissuade the locals from using his surgery as a drop-in centre and finds himself forced to deliver bad news to a former teacher already bitter at losing his job to Louisa. Adding to his woes, the decision to sack incompetent receptionist Elaine results in the entire village ostracising him. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A photographer is murdered shortly after exhibiting a compromising picture of Barnaby - who becomes a suspect in the ensuing investigation. Joyce concludes that someone is trying to frame him, and is stunned when he admits to a previous relationship with the murdered man's wife. Jones is forced to work with the irritating Martin Spellman to solve the case. Guest starring Liza Goddard, Adrian Scarborough and Richard Lintern

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson stars as the Parisian detective in this drama based on the novels of Georges Simenon. The summer of 1955 finds Paris in the grip of both sweltering heat and terror on the streets. Four women have been murdered in Montmartre, with no apparent connection between the victims. Chief Inspector Maigret comes under huge pressure from the public and his superiors to apprehend the killer before he can strike again. Fiona Shaw and Mark Heap head up the supporting cast

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: The battle of Waterloo gets underway, with George fighting in the thick of it. Amelia fears for her husband's life, while Jos is far more worried about himself, despite being miles from the front line. Becky sees an opportunity to make a profit from the chaos of war by selling Rawdon's horses - knowing that the cowardly Jos will pay her anything for a way to escape

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells the police about Don's confession. Jim and Andy try to keep the news of Fiona's engagement from Steve. Angie meets Chris and takes an instant fancy to him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony is confident his new business will be a success. Ned tells Jan that he needs her back home. Roy tells Kelly he knows she's pregnant and offers to help

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara is attacked by an unknown assailant while she is in bed. Biff tells Ned that Alex is back and they head to Oakwell Hall to make him pay for Linda's death

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Chaos reigns after an unknown sniper shoots a woman as she leaves a meeting of the flower show committee. DS Rachel Dawson's enquiries reveal the gunman may have missed his intended target - and when Blaketon spots an article in the local paper, he realises the bullet might have been aimed at him. Meanwhile, Peggy buys an ice-cream van to take advantage of the hot weather, but makes the mistake of leaving David in charge of the project. Lindsey Coulson guests alongside Derek Fowlds and Clare Wille

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Comedy series. When a storm makes him homeless again, Wilfred has to find a new dwelling

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: David Jason stars as Pop Larkin, the country-dwelling bumpkin with a heart of gold for whom everything has to be just 'perfick', with Pam Ferris as Ma Larkin and Catherine Zeta-Jones in her breakthrough role as the couple's voluptuous eldest daughter Mariette. Philip Franks also stars as the tax inspector who finds himself drawn into the family's easy-going world

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry and Louisa return to England. While his mother is reunited with her cousins, Larry returns to London, where he falls in with a group of Bohemian artists - including writer Henry Miller. Back in Corfu, Leslie gets offered a job as a policeman, Gerry saves a donkey from an abusive farmer, and Spiro forbids Margo from seeing Zoltan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobody's taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day one of the Winter Festival, including the 3.00 Ladbrokes Long Distance Hurdle, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.35. With analysis from Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tries to help Sam to read. Biff tells Kim about Steve's deal with Alex. Kelly tells Chris that she is pregnant. Heather and Lyn accuse Eric of swindling Outhwaite

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is fuming with Steve over his involvement with Alex. Chris tells Kelly he will help her get an abortion. Lyn finds Outhwaite unconscious on the floor of one of the barns

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Chris spends the night on Angie's sofa. Steve is convinced Fiona will marry him and not Alan. Mike plans to record Don confessing to starting the fire

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don tells Ashley he was joking about setting fire to the factory. Vera worries that Tricia and Ray are getting serious. Jim tells Andy he must stop feeling sorry for himself

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The medic struggles to dissuade the locals from using his surgery as a drop-in centre and finds himself forced to deliver bad news to a former teacher already bitter at losing his job to Louisa. Adding to his woes, the decision to sack incompetent receptionist Elaine results in the entire village ostracising him. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Carry On Henry (1971)
programme.desc: King Henry VIII plots to rid himself of his new French wife when her craving for garlic becomes too much to cope with. However, he cannot afford to anger the king of France, and tries to make it look as if his queen has had an affair. Comedy, starring Sid James, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Terry Scott, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Leanne tells Imran that with Steve now intent on stopping her aappeal, she wants to sue the hospital for negligence. Jenny urges Johnny to leave the past behind, and Adam hopes to reconcile with Sarah

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A photographer is murdered shortly after exhibiting a compromising picture of Barnaby - who becomes a suspect in the ensuing investigation. Joyce concludes that someone is trying to frame him, and is stunned when he admits to a previous relationship with the murdered man's wife. Jones is forced to work with the irritating Martin Spellman to solve the case. Guest starring Liza Goddard, Adrian Scarborough and Richard Lintern

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson stars as the Parisian detective in this drama based on the novels of Georges Simenon. The summer of 1955 finds Paris in the grip of both sweltering heat and terror on the streets. Four women have been murdered in Montmartre, with no apparent connection between the victims. Chief Inspector Maigret comes under huge pressure from the public and his superiors to apprehend the killer before he can strike again. Fiona Shaw and Mark Heap head up the supporting cast

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: The battle of Waterloo gets underway, with George fighting in the thick of it. Amelia fears for her husband's life, while Jos is far more worried about himself, despite being miles from the front line. Becky sees an opportunity to make a profit from the chaos of war by selling Rawdon's horses - knowing that the cowardly Jos will pay her anything for a way to escape
