programme.desc: The warlord is sent by his mother to capture a legendary monster for her petting zoo, and takes Jack and Tashi along with him

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys start their own printing press, but end up making up their own stories to stay one step ahead of the town crier

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: A domestic gander joins the flock after saving the geese from a wolf

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: The flock are all playing a game, except Golden Eye who prefers to get ready to meet goose-star Jack Hunk

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A woman redevelops her flat in west London, striving to achieve a high level of finish with a modest budget in the hope of making it more appealing to potential tenants. Sarah Beeny presents

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The consultants explain how changes in diet could help a 19-year-old dancer deal with Crohn's disease and boost the fertility of a man desperate to become a father. They also help a teenager redefine her relationship with food following gastric bypass surgery. Pixie McKenna examines the effectiveness of energy drinks, and a nationwide survey contributes to research into a natural remedy for insomnia

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Hitler's deputy, Rudolf Hess, the third in line to leadership of Nazi Germany, who was declared insane by the Fuehrer after making an unexpected errand of peace to Britain in 1941. The senior Nazi's fitness to stand trial was at the centre of his case in the tribunal. Narrated by Matthew MacFadyen and starring Ben Cross as Hess

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: The house is rocked by controversy when the publication of a scandalous novel exposes Blanche's unconventional lifestyle, bringing the doctor face to face with the author of the book and her former lover Lady Portia Alresford. But when the passion between the pair reignites, the doctor becomes conflicted. Meanwhile, Persie reveals a shocking secret to Hallam, and Beryl's workload prompts her to take a stand - until a familiar old face appears to offer a few wise words. Emilia Fox guest stars, with Alex Kingston, Jean Marsh, Ed Stoppard and Claire Foy

programme.title: Malachi's Cove (1973)
programme.desc: A 14-year-old girl in a 19th-century Cornish village is left to fend for herself after her parents die. She finds a way to support herself and her wheelchair-bound grandfather by gathering seaweed along the coast and selling it. Family drama, starring Donald Pleasence, Veronica Quilligan, Peter Vaughan and Arthur English

programme.title: For the Love of Ada (1972)
programme.desc: A pensioner goes to great lengths to celebrate her wedding anniversary. Comedy, based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Irene Handl and Wilfred Pickles

programme.title: Jamal
programme.desc: A Rastafarian explains his philosophy whilst growing and cultivating an orchard on a small Caribbean island

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Davies helps the Dwyers, a couple who want to open a music school in southern France, find a suitable chateau in the £250,000 price range

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home

programme.title: Our Queen
programme.desc: Feature-length documentary filmed throughout 2012, providing an in-depth portrait of Elizabeth II during her Diamond Jubilee year. Prince Charles and other members of the royal family share their stories, providing insights into the Queen's life over an important and busy 12 months filled with celebrations. The programme also reveals what really happens when the prime minister sits down with the monarch, and the lid is lifted on that memorable 007 moment at the London Olympic Games opening ceremony

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Hitler's deputy, Rudolf Hess, the third in line to leadership of Nazi Germany, who was declared insane by the Fuehrer after making an unexpected errand of peace to Britain in 1941. The senior Nazi's fitness to stand trial was at the centre of his case in the tribunal. Narrated by Matthew MacFadyen and starring Ben Cross as Hess

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s, telling the story of swing. Featuring archive footage and contributions from Frank Sinatra, Bing Crosby, Benny Goodman and Woody Herman

programme.title: Last Passenger (2013)
programme.desc: A widowed doctor travelling by train with his son realises that the mysterious driver has cut the brakes and they are speeding toward a fatal collision. With the aid of four of his fellow passengers, they try to find a way to avert the disaster. Thriller, starring Dougray Scott, Kara Tointon and Lindsay Duncan

programme.title: Brothers' Day (2015)
programme.desc: A Manchester teenager feels abandoned by his older brother, who has turned to a life of crime. However, when his latest deal ends badly, the younger sibling finds his eyes opened to the impossible choices his brother faces every day. Crime drama, starring Tom Collins and Lewis Fletcher

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The coach reaches Poitiers on the French leg of the tour, where there is a chance to explore a chateau. The travellers also visit an animal park, before turning their attention to the forthcoming vote

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A south London couple gamble their savings in an attempt to raise enough cash to buy their dream home in the countryside. The plan is to renovate their two-bedroom former council flat so they can make money by renting it out for as much as possible. Property developer Sarah Beeny keeps an eye on their progress

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb gives her expert opinion to British house-hunter Adrian Wilkes, who is searching for a property on the Caribbean island of Grenada, where he hopes to purchase the perfect holiday home for £60,000

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: House-hunters Craig and Roseanne Milby receive advice from international property expert Fay Davies about buying a dream holiday home on Spain's Almeria coast

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby challenges Vic to a climbing contest, with the winner joining the crew of the Drakkar

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Faxe steals sausages from an Icelandic king, which results in the whole crew getting held to ransom

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra opens a restaurant, but encounters problems with missing food and a sleepy chef

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra tries to discover who has been taking his vegetables

programme.title: Rockets in the Dunes (1960)
programme.desc: A Devon beach is used by local children for racing sand yachts, but their fun may be at an end when the army plan to use the dunes as a training ground. The youngsters rally the local community to save their beach, but the discovery of disused mines complicates things further. Family drama, starring Richard Adam and Fiona Davie

programme.title: The Getting of Wisdom (1977)
programme.desc: A snobbish Victorian college for young ladies is thrown into disarray by the arrival of a strong-willed girl from the Outback. Australian drama, starring Barry Humphries, Susannah Fowle, John Waters and Terence Donovan

programme.title: Portrait from Life (1949)
programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling

programme.title: Maria Marten: Or, Murder in the Red Barn (1935)
programme.desc: An evil squire lures a woman into marriage, but when she becomes pregnant, he decides to murder her, leaving a young gypsy who is in love with her to take the blame. Period drama, starring Tod Slaughter and Sophie Stewart

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Wapping for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Davies advises opera-lover Rosie Wilson and her daughter on purchasing a second home in Tuscany with wonderful views of the countryside on a budget of £300,000

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The consultants explain how changes in diet could help a 19-year-old dancer deal with Crohn's disease and boost the fertility of a man desperate to become a father. They also help a teenager redefine her relationship with food following gastric bypass surgery. Pixie McKenna examines the effectiveness of energy drinks, and a nationwide survey contributes to research into a natural remedy for insomnia

programme.title: The Ivy
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes as eminent restaurant The Ivy undergoes its first major renovation in 25 years. The programme follows director Fernando Peire and executive chef Gary Lee as they oversee the London institution's re-launch. Features interviews with clientele, including Calvin Klein, Dame Joan Collins, Maggi Hambling, Ronald Harwood, Tracey Emin and Julian Fellowes

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: On the eve of the withdrawal from Afghanistan of most American, British and other Nato forces, Panorama examines a Taliban stronghold just 60 miles from the capital Kabul. Nagieb Khaja, who has reported from Afghanistan for 10 years, reveals a hidden and dangerous world, where strict sharia law is enforced. It is from here Taliban forces are now targeting the capital itself

programme.title: Death Trench (2017)
programme.desc: At the end of the First World War, a squad of soldiers investigates an abandoned German underground base. They soon face a fight for survival against the victims of an experimental biological weapon, who have been transformed into frenzied bloodthirsty killers. Horror, starring Rossif Sutherland and Robert Stadlober

programme.title: London Town (2016)
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy living in 1970s London has his life changed forever when his is introduced to The Clash by his estranged mother. Drama, starring Tom Hughes, Jonathan Rhys Meyers and Dougray Scott

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: On the eve of the withdrawal from Afghanistan of most American, British and other Nato forces, Panorama examines a Taliban stronghold just 60 miles from the capital Kabul. Nagieb Khaja, who has reported from Afghanistan for 10 years, reveals a hidden and dangerous world, where strict sharia law is enforced. It is from here Taliban forces are now targeting the capital itself

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny meets a Tonbridge family who have resigned themselves to life on a building site as they attempt to modernise and extend their three-bedroom bungalow in the hope of moving up the property ladder

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Patsy Emmerson hope to buy a dream holiday home in Gran Canaria for £100,000. They check out a beachside apartment, a country house, a hillside villa and a luxury retreat. Presented by Amanda Lamb

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb helps a couple searching for a holiday home on what is arguably Europe's most glamorous stretch of coast - the south of France's Cote d'Azur. A waterfront apartment in Port Grimaud and two stylish village houses are among the properties considered

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps Wiltshire couple and pop opera fanatics Nikki Kedge and Angela Rawson find a bigger home in their dream location of Scotland's west coast. Desperate for more room to put on plays for local children, they view six houses in the county of Dumfries and Galloway - but will there be anything that tickles their fancy?

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a Bristol-based family longing for the open spaces and breathtaking countryside offered by the east coast of Scotland. The hunt for their dream property takes them more than 60 miles along the coastline, visiting Dundee, renowned for its marmalade and 400-million-year-old volcano, the harbour town of Montrose and Stonehaven, home to Dunnottar Castle

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett selects memorable moments from the house-hunting series as she returns to some of her favourite locations. From Woolacombe to Whitby, and Margate to the Isle of Wight, the retrospective illustrates why the English coastline has become the latest property hotspot

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home

programme.title: Supersonic Saucer (1956)
programme.desc: A group of children help protect a stranded alien from criminals who want to exploit his strange powers. Sci-fi adventure, starring Marcia Manolescue and Gillian Harrison

programme.title: The Kid From Canada (1958)
programme.desc: A boy visits his family in the Scottish Highlands, where his boastfulness gets him into trouble during a horse riding trip. Family drama, with Christopher Braden and Bernard Braden

programme.title: For the Love of Ada (1972)
programme.desc: A pensioner goes to great lengths to celebrate her wedding anniversary. Comedy, based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Irene Handl and Wilfred Pickles

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: The Wind of Change (1961)
programme.desc: An unemployed teenager in Notting Hill grows increasingly frustrated with his life, his anger manifesting as a deep-seated racism. When he and his friends commit a brutal act of violence on a black man and his white girlfriend, the attack has devastating repercussions for his family. Drama, starring Donald Pleasence and Johnny Briggs

programme.title: King Willy
programme.desc: A portrait of the famous old Hampstead pub William IV. Observe the action and hear from a number of regulars about their experiences and enjoyment of this establishment

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Fay Davies helps an Oxford woman hoping to relocate to Spain find the ideal apartment in Barcelona

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: London-based current affair series, with Michael Hayman

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home

programme.title: Modern Times
programme.desc: Documentary about the characters who live and work in the area of central London, which is famous for being the most coveted square on the Monopoly board and is home to Claridge's hotel and Christie's auction house. The film reveals a world of surprises and deep contrasts, including an estate agent selling multimillion-pound mansions, cafe owners hanging on to their livelihoods by their fingernails, an NHS doctor whose patients range from billionaires to people on benefits, an upmarket resident who dresses in Victorian clothing and a cabbie living in social housing

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Exploring the London's surprising and secret wildlife, including seals, parakeets, badgers, foxes and even pelicans who have made London their home. The programme also focuses on the admirers of these animals, from Billingsgate fish porters and Indian chefs to Crayfish Bob, who scours the canals for foreign species

programme.title: Fingersmith
programme.desc: As Maud's wedding day approaches, Sue struggles to hide both her love for her mistress and her guilt over Rivers' plan to send his new wife to an asylum once they are married, but cannot stop the scheme from going ahead. When the ceremony is over, a shocking turn of events puts her in a terrifying situation that she could never have foreseen. Drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Rupert Evans, Elaine Cassidy and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Last Passenger (2013)
programme.desc: A widowed doctor travelling by train with his son realises that the mysterious driver has cut the brakes and they are speeding toward a fatal collision. With the aid of four of his fellow passengers, they try to find a way to avert the disaster. Thriller, starring Dougray Scott, Kara Tointon and Lindsay Duncan

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: Modern Times
programme.desc: Documentary about the characters who live and work in the area of central London, which is famous for being the most coveted square on the Monopoly board and is home to Claridge's hotel and Christie's auction house. The film reveals a world of surprises and deep contrasts, including an estate agent selling multimillion-pound mansions, cafe owners hanging on to their livelihoods by their fingernails, an NHS doctor whose patients range from billionaires to people on benefits, an upmarket resident who dresses in Victorian clothing and a cabbie living in social housing

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Presenter Amanda Lamb accompanies mother and daughter Carol and Helen Giles to Florida's Gulf Coast in search of a house for £100,000. Among the properties featured are a three-bedroom detached house and a beachfront apartment

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Teacher Sarah dreams of climbing Mount Kilimanjaro, but first she must take on the mighty Cube, while pizza delivery man Tony reckons he's got what it takes to beat it. Tackling a series of increasingly difficult challenges, the contestants are under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £250,000. Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman put an 'international' spin on their unorthodox quiz show, by inviting famous faces from all over the world to team up. Seeking the least likely correct answers today are American singers Jimmy Osmond and Kym Mazelle, Irish pop duo Jedward, Singapore-born chef Nancy Lam and Australian comedian Sam Simmons, and Argentinean football coach Ossie Ardiles and Spanish sports journalist Guillem Balagué. Plus, Jimmy Osmond treats the presenter duo to a special performance
